title: Androsace × aretioides
intro: Androsace × aretioides, also known as Rock Jasmine or Alpine Rose, is a hybrid flowering plant species in the Primulaceae family, characterized by its pink or white, bell-shaped flowers and compact, ground-covering growth habit. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Androsace × aretioides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoid glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Androsace × aretioides is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used as a tincture, to harness its therapeutic benefits.
taxonomy: Androsace × aretioides is a hybrid species of flowering plant. It belongs to the family Primulaceae. This hybrid is a cross between the Alpine Rock Jasmine (Androsace alpina) and the Areti Rock Jasmine (Androsace aretioides). The classification of Androsace × aretioides is as a nothospecies within the genus Androsace. The hybrid's classification reflects its unique characteristics and ancestry within the Primulaceae family.
